prompt:
Assign GRADE certainty ratings to each evidence item below.
Ratings: HIGH (systematic review of RCTs, low heterogeneity), MODERATE (RCT with some concerns, or consistent observational), LOW (observational studies, high heterogeneity, indirect evidence), VERY_LOW (case reports, expert opinion, serious limitations).

For each item, output ONLY the number and rating, one per line:
1. HIGH
2. MODERATE
...

EVIDENCE:
1. [SILVER] Source: The Impact of the Lamination Process on the Adhesion Propert | Statement: PET/Al backsheet with TPO dropped from 160 N/cm to 33-45 N/cm after 3000h damp heat at 85C/85%RH
2. [SILVER] Source: The Impact of the Lamination Process on the Adhesion Propert | Statement: Failure mode changed from backsheet-encapsulant interface delamination to backsheet sub-layer delamination after damp heat for PET backsheets
3. [SILVER] Source: Degradation Study of the Peel Strength of Mini-Modules Under | Statement: Activation energy for adhesion degradation between backsheet and encapsulant is approximately 51 kJ/mol via Arrhenius analysis
4. [SILVER] Source: Degradation Study of the Peel Strength of Mini-Modules Under | Statement: Temperature and RH both accelerate adhesion degradation, with temperature acceleration rate faster than humidity
5. [SILVER] Source: Degradation Study of the Peel Strength of Mini-Modules Under | Statement: Kinetic model describes adhesion degradation rate as proportional to moisture concentration at the interface with Arrhenius temperature dependence
